acceptance_targets: [CH11_CRAFT_DREHER_ESCALATION] By the time the revised text reaches snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:71, a cold reader can name the specific corroboration or clock that forces Anya to escalate on Dreher before testing Falk; if the only explanation is "he seemed scared and secretive," this finding remains unresolved.

[CH11_AWARD_SECOND_HALF_STALL] By the end of the revised chapter at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:209, the reader can state one new actionable fact, cost, or decision tied to Falk beyond "he might be the mole" and "time is short"; if the ending still only restates ambiguity and urgency, this finding fails.

[CH11_PROSE_OVERSTATEMENT_AFTER_ARRIVAL] After the revised reveal around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:79 and after the revised phone exchange around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:165, the immediately following paragraph must add a new consequence, action, or interpersonal pressure; if those paragraphs can still be summarized mainly as "telepathy isn't truth," "she violated privacy," or "their honesty has limits," this finding remains unresolved.
[RED-003] Ch 11 does not re-state thinning hair, reading glasses, cardigan, or institutional-furniture metaphor from Ch 1. The pen anecdote, if retained, adds demonstrably new information.
[RED-015] Ch 11's bathroom scene does not contain 'flushed the toilet she hadn't used' or synonymous phrasing from Ch 3.
[RED-027] Ch 11 em-dash-containing prose lines are at or below 8%.
[RED-038] Ch 11 'She'-opener sentences are at or below 7%.

[LW05-002] By the end of chapter 11, the reader should be able to explain why Anya stayed on the mole hunt first and what risk she believes she is accepting by leaving Yor's crisis untouched for three days.

[LW05-003] When chapters 10 through 12 are read back-to-back, chapter 11 should register first as the wrong-answer chapter, not as the third consecutive chapter recapitulating that telepathy violates trust.

findings.acceptance_test: By the time the revised text reaches snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:71, a cold reader can name the specific corroboration or clock that forces Anya to escalate on Dreher before testing Falk; if the only explanation is "he seemed scared and secretive," this finding remains unresolved.
findings.problem: The chapter asks the reader to accept a formal counterintelligence referral before it has supplied either a decisive non-telepathic corroboration or a clearly stated reason Anya cannot spend one more pass on Falk first. Because the false accusation is the hinge of the chapter, the thin on-page "why now" makes her misstep feel partially author-arranged instead of tragically plausible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen the evidence chain before the memo at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:45, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:67, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:71. Either add one concrete observable anomaly that pushes Dreher above ordinary concealment, or state a specific immediate risk that makes waiting to read Falk untenable. Keep the accusation wrong, but make it wrong for reasons a cold reader can fully track.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised chapter at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:209, the reader can state one new actionable fact, cost, or decision tied to Falk beyond "he might be the mole" and "time is short"; if the ending still only restates ambiguity and urgency, this finding fails.
findings.problem: After the Dreher reveal lands, the back half mostly certifies the same realization instead of generating a comparably sharp second turn. Falk yields no new actionable proof, the memo is explicitly framed as non-evidence, and Damian's call resets the clock rather than materially advancing the mole hunt. For a two-scene compressed-force chapter, that leaves the second movement reading like thematic aftermath rather than a fresh chapter-level escalation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the Falk/phone-call movement at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:131, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:165, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:193 so it creates one new operational turn before bed: a specific suspicious irregularity tied to Falk, a decision Anya makes because of the memo, or a concrete resource/public consequence that changes tomorrow's hunt. Keep Falk unconfirmed, but do not end on the same investigative state plus a louder deadline.

findings.acceptance_test: After the revised reveal around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:79 and after the revised phone exchange around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:165, the immediately following paragraph must add a new consequence, action, or interpersonal pressure; if those paragraphs can still be summarized mainly as "telepathy isn't truth," "she violated privacy," or "their honesty has limits," this finding remains unresolved.
findings.problem: The chapter keeps paraphrasing its own lesson after the concrete beats have already made it legible. Dreher's diagnosis, Falk's unreadable calm, and Anya's flat "I don't know" each land cleanly, then the narration translates them into abstract thesis language about truth, identity, institutional betrayal, and what the alliance "actually sounded like." That repeated moral/thematic paraphrase blunts the force of the specific scenes and makes the prose feel self-explanatory instead of controlled.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or recast the abstract summaries at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:87, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:89, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:135, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:197, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:205, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:207. Once the reveal at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:79 and the uncertainty beat at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_11.md:125 have landed, advance through concrete consequence, physical behavior, or a new choice rather than re-stating the epistemological thesis in cleaner language.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 11 does not re-state thinning hair, reading glasses, cardigan, or institutional-furniture metaphor from Ch 1. The pen anecdote, if retained, adds demonstrably new information.
findings.evidence: Ch 1 line 161: 'Thinning hair, reading glasses, a cardigan that was either brown or grey... blended into any institutional setting like he'd been issued with the furniture... Eden Academy graduation... fountain pen — silver, heavy, with her initials'; Ch 11 line 2823: 'thinning hair, reading glasses... three decades at desks... grey cardigan most days'; Ch 11 line 2825: 'Eden Academy graduation ceremony... fountain pen. Silver barrel... initials engraved... For signing important things'
findings.problem: Falk is fully introduced in Ch 1 (line 161) with thinning hair, reading glasses, institutional-furniture metaphor, grey cardigan, and the graduation pen anecdote. Ch 11 (lines 2823-2825) re-introduces him with the same descriptor cluster AND retells the pen anecdote with expanded detail. This is a full re-introduction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the physical re-description at Ch 11 line 2823. Enter through Anya's changed relationship to Falk (suspicion, the mole investigation) rather than re-teaching his appearance. The pen anecdote retelling at line 2825 may stay only if it delivers new emotional leverage not present in Ch 1.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 11's bathroom scene does not contain 'flushed the toilet she hadn't used' or synonymous phrasing from Ch 3.
findings.evidence: Ch 3 line 605: 'Anya flushed the toilet she hadn't used, washed her hands she didn't need to wash, and went to get coffee'; Ch 11 line 2815: 'She flushed the toilet she hadn't used. Washed her hands.'
findings.problem: Near-verbatim bathroom-break sequence appears in both Ch 3 and Ch 11. Same structure: flushed a toilet she hadn't used, washed hands she didn't need to wash. Reads as a repeated scene-exit template.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the Ch 11 exit from the bathroom to avoid echoing the Ch 3 phrasing. A different physical action or transition would prevent the reader from experiencing deja vu.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2702; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2704; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2708; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2723; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2725; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2733

evidence.excerpt: 2702: *The code. Our code. The Garden — on his records. Nine contracts. Four years.*

2704: And beneath the words, in the layer where thought became feeling and feeling became the thing Anya had been reading in people her entire life: the slow structural crack of a woman realizing that the foundation she'd built twenty years of killing upon might not hold her weight anymore. Not the shock of new information but the nauseating click of a pattern half-glimpsed before — the shipping clerk three months ago, the invoices that didn't match, the handler's too-quick reassurance — assembling into a picture she'd been refusing to see.

2708: She did this to everyone. She'd done it to Damian in a safe house two nights ago, reading the unguarded center of him while he kissed her. She'd done it to Sylvia, absorbing trust while delivering a lie. She did it to her father every time his worry leaked past his careful composure. And now she'd done it to her mother — who was walking alone down a dark road with blood drying on her hands and a crack spreading through the story she'd been telling herself for two decades — and Anya had caught every tremor of that crack because that was what she was. Not what she chose to be. What she was.
2723: Anya had spent three days inside WISE headquarters, and her head hurt in a way that was becoming structural.

2725: Not the sharp spike of telepathic overload — a crowded reception, hundreds of minds pressing in at once. That was acute and temporary, like staring into bright light. This was different. Three days of reading her own colleagues one at a time, in engineered encounters she manufactured throughout the building, each requiring sustained close-range attention while pretending she was doing something ordinary. Waiting seven minutes by the third-floor elevator for a specific person to leave their desk. Timing her coffee run to coincide with someone else's. Standing near a conversation pretending to look at her phone, except the conversation was inside someone's skull and the phone was a prop and the hovering was the most invasive thing she had ever done on purpose.

2733: So she'd started the other way. Paper first, the way Loid had taught her since she was sixteen: before you use the cheat, do the work. Access logs. Communication intercepts. Briefing attendance records cross-referenced against the timeline of specific intelligence that had reached the Renewal Front's operational pipeline. Which WISE operations had the Front anticipated, and who had been briefed on those operations beforehand? Grosz's intelligence — the picture of the Front's military-civilian structure and defense-contract funding channels that Damian had relayed to her — narrowed the question further. If the mole was passing intelligence that served the Front's specific procurement and positioning needs, the access pattern should overlap with those channels.

findings.problem: Chapter 10 ends with Anya hearing that the Garden's code appears in Dominic's records and with Yor internally cracking under that realization, but chapter 11 jumps straight to 'three days inside WISE headquarters' without any on-page triage of why the mole hunt outranks the Yor/Garden emergency. In sequence, the prior chapter's family-operational collision feels topic-switched away rather than consciously shelved.
findings.rewrite_direction:
Add a short bridge at the start of chapter 11, or in its first investigative beat, clarifying why Anya cannot act on what she read from Yor yet: no physical evidence, no safe way to approach Yor, and a shorter clock on the WISE leak tied to Damian's warning.

Aggregator guidance:
- Cross-chapter context: Ch 10 ends with Anya telepathically reading Yor's discovery that the Garden appears in Dominic's subcontracting records (nine contracts, four years). Yor is privately cracking under this realization. Ch 11 jumps to 'three days inside WISE headquarters' without triaging why the mole hunt outranks the Yor/Garden emergency. The bridge should establish: Anya cannot act on Yor's intelligence (no physical evidence she can show, no safe way to approach Yor without revealing telepathy, and the WISE mole has a shorter operational clock tied to Damian's warning about an imminent Renewal Front provocation).

findings.acceptance_test: When chapters 10 through 12 are read back-to-back, chapter 11 should register first as the wrong-answer chapter, not as the third consecutive chapter recapitulating that telepathy violates trust.

evidence.excerpt: 2708: She did this to everyone. She'd done it to Damian in a safe house two nights ago, reading the unguarded center of him while he kissed her. She'd done it to Sylvia, absorbing trust while delivering a lie. She did it to her father every time his worry leaked past his careful composure. And now she'd done it to her mother — who was walking alone down a dark road with blood drying on her hands and a crack spreading through the story she'd been telling herself for two decades — and Anya had caught every tremor of that crack because that was what she was. Not what she chose to be. What she was.

2710: The violation wasn't romantic this time. It wasn't operational. It was the same violation it had always been, the one that didn't change shape depending on who she committed it against: structural, constant, built into the fact of her existence, committed against everyone she loved by a power she hadn't asked for and had never once been given consent to use. Damian's unguarded feelings in a safe house and Yor's private grief on a service road. The scale differed. The mechanism was identical.

2807: She had read him perfectly. Every fragment of his surface thoughts was accurate. The fear, the concealment, the secret meetings, the cash payments, the timeline — all real. All true. All pointing toward the thing he was hiding. Her telepathy hadn't given her a wrong answer. It had given her exactly what Martin Dreher was thinking about, which was his own death, and she had looked at a man drowning in the most private terror of his life and diagnosed treason.

2809: Surface thoughts were not truth. They were not intentions, not identity, not the reason underneath the feeling. They were what a person was afraid of at any given moment, and people were afraid of what haunted them, not what defined them. A dying man's anxiety and a traitor's anxiety wore the same face. Her telepathy couldn't tell the difference because there was no difference — not on the surface, and the surface was all she had ever been able to read.

2855: She sat at her desk in the bullpen's overhead hum and understood, with a clarity that felt more like nausea than insight, what she had been doing for the past week. She had been reading her colleagues' minds — their fears, their frustrations, their private medical crises, their small grievances and buried shames — without their knowledge or permission, to identify a person who was betraying WISE's institutional trust through an undisclosed advantage. And she was conducting this investigation as someone who had been betraying that same institutional trust since her first day: through a power she had never disclosed, accountable to no one, appointed by no one, performing surveillance that no one had sanctioned or could review. She was the organization's secret internal eye, and she had been wrong about Dreher, and she couldn't confirm Falk, and the only certainty the week had produced was that she had violated the privacy of four people who trusted her and gotten nothing usable in return.
3290: She had been inside Becky's thoughts for their entire friendship.

3302: She didn't say it because Becky needed the friend she believed Anya was. Because the truth would destroy the one relationship Becky trusted — the one person she believed saw her clearly. And the destruction would be worse because the betrayal wasn't a single act. It wasn't a lie that could be confessed and forgiven. It was a condition. Every day of their friendship, every conversation, every phone call, every silence — all of it, retroactively poisoned. There was no version of the truth that left the friendship standing.

findings.problem: Read consecutively, chapters 10, 11, and 12 all stage the same function: Anya receives unconsented access to someone's private pain and then reframes telepathy as a universal violation against people who trust her. Chapter 10 already broadens the Damian beat to 'everyone she loves,' and chapter 12 personalizes it through Becky. Chapter 11 therefore repeats the moral theorem instead of letting its distinctive contribution be the operational lesson that telepathy can produce a costly false positive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep Dreher's misread and Falk's opacity, but compress one or both guilt-summing paragraphs in chapter 11 and spend that space on changed investigative behavior, sharper mole pressure, or a more concrete consequence from the wasted two days.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise blends comedy, espionage action, and genuine emotional stakes. The creative risk is in honoring all three simultaneously without letting any one mode cannibalize the others. Do not soften the violence because the novel is funny. Do not suppress the comedy because the stakes are real. Do not retreat from Anya's moral complicity (she reads minds without consent and uses it to manipulate) because she is the protagonist and we like her. The hardest scenes to get right are the ones where comedy and pain coexist — a fight that is also funny, a confession that is also absurd, a family dinner where love and deception are the same meal. Lean into those moments rather than resolving them into one register. The novel's best material lives in the dissonance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When violence, fear, grief, or moral compromise demands scene presence, render it with specificity. A bruise should have a color. A death should have weight. A lie should have a cost the liar feels in their body. The exception is Anya's telepathic restraint — she sometimes cannot bear to fully articulate what she's reading in someone's mind, and this selective omission is a character choice, not authorial squeamishness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in casual speech. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific register choices (Sylvia's professional diction, Damian's diplomatic mode). In contemporary casual scenes, an unjustified full-form line should be flagged and checked.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should share the same structural rhythm. Alternate between dialogue-heavy scenes and interiority-heavy passages. Vary scene count: two-scene chapters (11, 16, 17 — compressed force) against multi-scene chapters (8, 15 — breadth and momentum). Shift between comic dominant and dramatic dominant across the middle of the book, with the comic register maintaining presence even in dramatic chapters through Anya's voice and the Forger family's inherent absurdity. The tonal shift at Chapter 13 is a deliberate gear change, not a gradual dimming — comedy should be fully alive in Chapter 12 and markedly reduced (not absent) in Chapter 13. The Chapter 13 POV shift to Damian is a structural event: the reader's relationship to information changes. The final act (14-17) maintains Anya's voice and the family's warmth as comic undertone, but the dominant mode is drama with consequences. Chapter 17's dinner scene reintroduces comedy through a register the novel has not previously accessed — Damian's self-conscious thought management around a known telepath — which only works because it follows sixteen chapters of oblivious thought-reading.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary and accessible, not literary-precious. The prose should have energy and forward drive. Humor comes from observation, character logic, and the gap between surface and interior — not from clever narration or authorial commentary. Let the funny things be funny without signposting them. Let the sad things sit without interpretation. The prose should be invisible when it needs to be and vivid when the scene demands it, but never decorative for its own sake.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: the prose commenting on its own comedy, winking at the reader, or admiring a character's wit. The humor lives in the situation, not in the telling.

Telepathy-as-omniscience: Anya's mind-reading should feel like cognitive overload, not like a narrator's convenient access to exposition. She reads surface thoughts, not deep motivations, and the thoughts she catches should be fragmentary, mundane, and sometimes misleading.

Prestige-creep: the prose drifting toward solemn literary register when the scene's natural mode is comic, warm, or chaotic. This novel's emotional power comes from the coexistence of comedy and genuine feeling, not from the comedy yielding to seriousness.

Briefing-itis: scenes where characters explain plot to each other or to the reader. Information should emerge through action, observation, and telepathic fragments — not through expository dialogue formatted as conversation.

Combat-poetry: fight scenes that become lyrical or aesthetically admiring. Violence should be fast, specific, physically grounded, and over quickly. Yor's lethality is matter-of-fact; Anya's fighting is scrappier and less elegant.

Emotion-labeling: telling the reader what a character feels instead of rendering the feeling through thought, action, and physical detail. 'She felt sad' is always worse than the specific thing that makes the sadness legible.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, primarily Anya. Chapter 7 (Damian), Chapter 10 (Yor), the Damian POV section in Chapter 13, and Damian's thread in Chapter 15 shift to close third on their respective POV characters. The telepathy layer is unique to Anya's POV — when she is the viewpoint character, other characters' surface thoughts appear as italicized interjections woven into the prose. In non-Anya POV chapters, no telepathic content appears. The Chapter 13 Damian POV section is structurally critical: it gives the reader access to Damian's interior at a depth Anya's telepathy cannot reach, and it reveals that he has identified Anya — knowledge the reader carries into the final act while Anya does not. This dramatic irony reversal is the novel's strongest structural surprise and depends on the reader experiencing Damian's depths unmediated by Anya's telepathic filter.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Action sequences: short sentences, concrete verbs, sensory immediacy. Comic scenes: longer sentences that build to turns, pacing that allows beats to land. Quiet domestic scenes: relaxed rhythm with room for observation and texture. Telepathic interjections should disrupt prose rhythm the way intrusive thoughts disrupt concentration — not ornamentally but as genuine cognitive interference. Avoid metronomic sentence patterns in any mode.
sensory_bias: visual surveillance — reading rooms, reading faces, the spy's hyperawareness of spatial arrangement and exit routes
auditory — overheard thoughts as a telepathic sixth sense layered over actual ambient sound
kinesthetic — action choreography, physical proximity, the body under stress or in intimacy
olfactory/gustatory — Yor's cooking, Berlint street food, the specific smell of institutional buildings and safe houses
